title: Method and control arrangement in a transportation surveillance system
abstract: Method (300) and control arrangement (110) of a transportation surveillance system (100), monitoring autonomous vehicles (105), for assisting a human operator in decision making concerning the monitored autonomous vehicles (105). The method (300) comprises detect- ing (301) an anomaly (160) within the transportation surveillance system (100); determining (302) a plurality of alternative actions to perform, in order to remedy, or at least reduce con- sequences of, the detected (301) anomaly (160); performing (303) a simulation of each of the determined (302) actions in a simulation model of the transportation surveillance system (100) comprising the detected (301) anomaly (160), to a predetermined future point in time; determining (304) outcome of each performed (303) simulation of the determined (302) ac- tions, at the predetermined future point in time; and outputting (306) results of the determined (304) respective outcome to the human operator.
claims:
Claims PATENT CLAIMS 1 . A method (300) in a control arrangement (1 10) of a transportation surveillance sys tem (100), monitoring a transportation system (200) comprising autonomous vehicles (105), for assisting a human operator in decision making concerning the monitored transportation system (200); which method (300) comprises: detecting (301 ) an anomaly (160) within the transportation system (200); determining (302) a plurality of alternative actions to perform, in order to remedy, or at least reduce consequences of, the detected (301 ) anomaly (160); performing (303) a simulation of each of the determined (302) actions in a simulation model of the transportation system (200) comprising the detected (301 ) anomaly (160), to a predetermined future point in time; determining (304) outcome of each performed (303) simulation of the determined (302) actions, at the predetermined future point in time; and outputting (306) results of the determined (304) respective outcome to the human operator. 2. The method (300) according to claim 1 , further comprising: converting (305) the determined (304) outcome of each performed (303) simulation into a common performance measurement, and wherein the outputted (306) results of the determined (304) respective outcome comprises the common performance measurement. 3. The method (300) according to any one of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the results of the determined (304) respective outcome is outputted (306) to the human operator at a user interface (130, 140a, 140b, 140c) situated in association with command instruments (150) for executing the action. 4. A control arrangement (1 10) of a transportation surveillance system (100), monitor ing a transportation system (200) comprising autonomous vehicles (105), for assisting a hu man operator in decision making concerning the monitored transportation system (200); wherein the control arrangement (1 10) is configured to: detect an anomaly (160) within the transportation system (200); determine a plurality of alternative actions to perform, in order to remedy, or at least reduce consequences of, the detected anomaly (160); perform a simulation of each of the determined actions in a simulation model of the transportation system (200) comprising the detected anomaly (160), to a predetermined fu ture point in time; 

 determine outcome of each performed simulation of the determined actions, at the predetermined future point in time; and output, via a user interface (130, 140a, 140b, 140c), results of the determined re spective outcome to the human operator. 5. The control arrangement (1 10) according to claim 4, further configured to convert the determined outcome of each performed simulation into a common per formance measurement, and output the results of the determined respective outcome in terms of the common performance measurement. 6. The control arrangement (1 10) according to any one of claim 4 or claim 5, further configured to output the results of the determined respective outcome is outputted to the human operator at a user interface (130, 140a, 140b, 140c) situated in association with com- mand instruments (150) for executing the action. 7. A computer program comprising program code for performing a method (300) ac cording to any of claims 1 -3, when the computer program is executed in a control unit (1 10) according to any of claims 4-6. 8. A transportation surveillance system (100) for monitoring a transportation system (200) comprising autonomous vehicles (105), which transportation surveillance system (100) comprises: a control arrangement (1 10) according to any one of claims 4-6; a sensor (120) for detecting an anomaly (160) within the transportation system (200); and a user interface (130, 140a, 140b, 140c), configured to output results of the deter mined respective outcome to the human operator.
